name: Equations
intro: So far we've mostly built terms affecting their arguments
in a uniform way. But what about distinguishing arguments?
The trick is almost always to put the argument in front of
some other terms and equate the computation result to the
Throughout this chapter, producing an equivalent term

content.name: Warm up
intro: Create a term that returns K for I and I for K

content.name: Identity upgrade
intro: Create a term that converts I to K, and leaves KI in place.
What it does to other terms is irrelevant. Multiple solutions are possible.

content.name: vice-
intro: f I = S, f S = K, and f K = I.

content.name: -versa
intro: f S = I, f I = K, and f K = S.
